tasks.title: Define project contracts
acceptance: Every environment check has a stable id, description, evidence source, severity, and either a command reference or a deterministic probe.
Adapter fallback is modeled as contract metadata, not executable truth.

checks: the template includes project profile, command registry, environment checks, blocking/warning severity, evidence source, and adapter fallback metadata.
project contracts schema validates the template
environment checks require evidence, severity, and command or probe source
adapter fallback is contract metadata only

tasks.title: Implement project env runner
acceptance: The runner validates the contract against `project-contracts.schema.json` before execution.
The runner executes command checks by resolving `commandRef` through `commandRegistry`.
The runner supports deterministic probes for file existence and executable availability.
The runner exits non-zero for failed blocking checks and exits zero when only warning checks fail.
The runner output includes check ids, severity, result, and evidence source.
The runner does not write `workflow-state.json`, `tasks.json`, or active plan files.

checks: test fixtures prove blocking failure, warning-only success, commandRef execution, and schema rejection.
runner validates the contract schema before executing checks
runner consumes commandRef and probe declarations from the contract
blocking and warning severities affect exit code correctly
runner is read-only for Harness runtime state

tasks.title: Wire project env runner
acceptance: `session-start.py` requires the runner, schema, and template as Harness assets but does not execute project environment checks.
`project-init` names the concrete output contract path and the runner command while preserving the contract-first rule.
`architecture.md` documents project contracts and the generic runner as separate from workflow state and session startup.

checks: documentation states contracts are the truth source and the runner does not replace `session-start.py`.
session-start gates runner/schema/template as Harness assets without executing project checks
project-init names the concrete contract output and runner command
architecture documents contracts as truth source and runner as read-only executor
